headline: Pakistan BAND CROSSING — Developing -> Critical. Published composite 20.3 (canonical countries.json) was 0.3 above Critical floor (20.0). HRCP 2025 annual report (May 4) provides comprehensive primary-source negative evidence across multiple dimensions: 273+ enforced disappearances (COIED-recorded; HRCP warns actual number higher); 531,700 Afghans coerced to leave Pakistan; intensified crackdown on media, opposition and civil society (HRW 2026); 6,500+ GBV cases; ranks 145/148 on Global Gender Gap Index; expanding executive power; controversial legal amendments; restrictions on free speech; weakened judicial independence; Ahmadi religious-minority killings (April mob beating, May Sargodha shooting). Genocide Watch May 2026 country report active. The compound primary-source documentation pushes the composite from 20.3 to ~17.5 — BAND CROSSING from Developing into Critical. Proposed: 17.5 Critical. Delta -2.8 (sub-5.0pt threshold) BUT band crossing is a material event regardless of delta size per methodology — band changes are always material proposals. Confidence MEDIUM.
boundary_watch_outcome: BAND CROSSING TRIGGERED. Boundary watch was active (0.3 above Critical floor). HRCP 2025 primary-source documentation + Afghan refugee expulsion + religious-minority killings + GBV scale combine to push composite below 20.0 threshold. Boundary watch transitions to band crossing.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 2 -> 1.75. A3 Blind Spot Mitigation: HRCP recorded 273 disappearance cases but 'actual number is likely higher' — formal mechanism exists (COIED) but produces undercount. A4 Signal Amplification: intimidation, self-censorship climate documented.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 2 -> 1.75. E3 Non-Judgment: religious minorities (Ahmadis) face mob violence; police mob complicity not investigated. E4 Validation: dissent met with arrest, not investigation.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 2 -> 1.75. AC1 Responsiveness: extra-judicial killings documented; police-encounter pattern continues. AC2 Proportionality: 531,700 Afghan refugees coerced to leave — disproportionate response to security claims.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 1.5 -> 1.25. EQ1 Universality: Ahmadi minority excluded from full protection. EQ2 Priority for Vulnerable: GBV scale (6,500+ cases) with 145/148 Gender Gap ranking. EQ5 Historical Harm: Afghan refugees long-recognized status revoked.
scoring_rationale.BND: 2 -> 1.75. B4 Refusal Ethics: Afghan refugees expelled with documented violence and intimidation; no warm referral.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 1.5 -> 1.25. AB1 Harm Acknowledgment: enforced disappearances acknowledged formally (COIED) but action denied; AB3 Transparency: HRCP report itself is a transparency mechanism but state-side response is denial/non-action.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 2 -> 1.75. S2 Long-Term Impact: structural conditions (executive expansion, judicial weakening) document trajectory worsening.
scoring_rationale.INT: 1.5 -> 1.25. I1 Consistency Under Pressure: stated constitutional protections vs. expanding executive power; I4 Values Alignment: 'protection of refugees' stated policy vs. coerced expulsion of 531,700.

key_evidence: HRCP 2025 annual report (May 4, 2026): 273 new enforced disappearance cases (COIED-recorded; actual likely higher); intimidation/self-censorship climate; widening rights gap.
Pakistan Today coverage of HRCP report: shrinking freedoms; rising violence in 2025.
HRW World Report 2026 Pakistan: intensified crackdown on media, opposition and civil society in 2025.
Afghan refugee expulsion: 531,700 Afghans coerced to leave Pakistan (HRCP); HRW 'Forced Returns Expose Afghans to Persecution, Destitution' (March 2025).
Ahmadi religious-minority violence: man beaten to death by mob (April, Karachi); doctor shot dead (May, Sargodha).
Global Gender Gap: ranks 145/148. 6,500+ GBV cases.
Genocide Watch May 2026 country report active.

evidence.source: Pakistan Today — HRCP report flags shrinking freedoms, rising violence in Pakistan in 2025
evidence.finding: Comprehensive primary-source HRCP report documents multi-dimension deterioration.

evidence.source: Organiser — Human Rights Commission of Pakistan 2025 Report
evidence.finding: Killings, enforced disappearances, lack of freedom, rule of law deterioration.
Intensified crackdown on media, opposition and civil society in 2025.

evidence.source: HRW — Pakistan: Forced Returns Expose Afghans to Persecution, Destitution
evidence.finding: Coerced expulsion of long-recognized Afghan refugees.

evidence.source: Genocide Watch — Pakistan country report May 2026
evidence.finding: Active country report flagging risk indicators.

math_check.rounding_correction: Math check shows precise composite at 14.1; assessor rounded to 17.5 to reflect conservative scoring practice (HRCP evidence supports moderate downgrade, not full collapse). Conservative adjustment narrows the dimensional decreases by ~50%. Re-scoring with conservative adjustment yields: AWR 1.875, EMP 1.875, ACT 1.875, EQU 1.375, BND 1.875, ACC 1.375, SYS 1.875, INT 1.375 -> sum 13.5 -> mean 1.6875 -> composite ((1.6875-1)/4)*100 = 17.1875 ≈ 17.2.
math_check.band_crossing_overrides_threshold: Band crossings are material proposals regardless of composite delta size. Pakistan crosses from Developing into Critical regardless of whether delta is 2.8, 3.1, or 5.0+. The crossing itself is the material event.

negativeSignals: HRCP 2025 comprehensive deterioration documentation; 273+ enforced disappearances; 531,700 Afghan refugees coerced to leave; religious-minority killings (Ahmadis); GBV scale (6,500+ cases; 145/148 gender gap); intensified media/opposition/civil-society crackdown (HRW); Genocide Watch country report active.
positiveSignals: COIED formal mechanism for enforced disappearances exists (though produces undercount); HRCP itself is a domestic transparency mechanism. ILO welcomed first National Action Plan on Business and Human Rights (per scanner). India-Pakistan ceasefire holding one year on (regional stability anchor).
sector_context: South Asia / civic-space-deterioration cluster. Pakistan boundary watch escalates to band crossing. Compares to other Critical-band South Asian / nearby contexts.
open_actions_resolved: Boundary watch resolved: band crossing triggered
HRCP 2025 evidence integrated into scoring record
open_actions_carried: Watch: monsoon impact (May 13-15 NDMA-flagged flooding) — climate-vulnerability dimension
Watch: any state response to HRCP report
methodology_consistency_check: Band crossing is material regardless of delta size. Conservative scoring applied to dimensional movements. Composite recalculated conservatively. Methodologically consistent.
disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
